title: Automatic Logoff — Stale Clinical Workstation Sessions

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.312(a)(2)(iii) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.15: Automatic Logoff — Stale Clinical Workstation Sessions.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.15' running on index=citrix session start/end, WinEventLog:Security 4800/4801 (workstation lock), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
spl:
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d
| eval start=strptime(SessionStart,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=if(isnotnull(SessionEnd), strptime(SessionEnd,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), now())
| eval session_hours=round((end-start)/3600,2)
| where session_hours>12 AND match(Application,"Hyperspace|Epic")
| stats max(session_hours) as max_hrs by UserName, client_ip, PublishedApplication
| sort - max_hrs
description: Identifies unusually long Citrix/VDI sessions and unlocked Windows consoles in patient care areas where automatic logoff may be misconfigured.
value: Identifies unusually long Citrix/VDI sessions and unlocked Windows consoles in patient care areas where automatic logoff may be misconfigured.
implementation: (1) Align with published idle timeout policy (e.g., 30 min); (2) tune for legitimate long shifts using department lookup; (3) feed findings to desktop engineering for GPO fixes.
visualization: Bar chart (max session by dept), Table (user, app, hours).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
grandmaExplanation: We spot unusually long Citrix/VDI sessions and unlocked Windows consoles in patient care areas where automatic logoff may be misconfigured — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=citrix` session start/end, `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801 (workstation lock).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align with published idle timeout policy (e.g., 30 min); (2) tune for legitimate long shifts using department lookup; (3) feed findings to desktop engineering for GPO fixes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d
| eval start=strptime(SessionStart,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=if(isnotnull(SessionEnd), strptime(SessionEnd,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), now())
| eval session_hours=round((end-start)/3600,2)
| where session_hours>12 AND match(Application,"Hyperspace|Epic")
| stats max(session_hours) as max_hrs by UserName, client_ip, PublishedApplication
| sort - max_hrs
```

Understanding this SPL

**Automatic Logoff — Stale Clinical Workstation Sessions (§164.312(a)(2)(iii))** — Identifies unusually long Citrix/VDI sessions and unlocked Windows consoles in patient care areas where automatic logoff may be misconfigured.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=citrix` session start/end, `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801 (workstation lock). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: citrix; **sourcetype**: citrix:session. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=citrix, sourcetype="citrix:session", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **start** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **end** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **session_hours** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where session_hours>12 AND match(Application,"Hyperspace|Epic")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by UserName, client_ip, PublishedApplication** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (max session by dept), Table (user, app, hours).
